stories.title: Pi-side dual-attribution reproducer harness
stories.goal: As a developer hardening the Pi-side drive lifecycle, I want a deterministic unit-test harness that replays the Drive 23/24 timing and signal sequence so that the dual-emission defect is reproduced in CI before the fix lands and is locked-in as a regression net after the fix lands.
definitionOfDone: New test fixture under tests/pi/obdii/drive/ replays Drive 23/24 timing against DriveDetector + lifecycle integration seam
Fixture is deterministic (no wall-clock dependency; injected clock or recorded-sample fixture)
Pre-fix run asserts the defect: DriveDetector emits 2 distinct drive_ids on same physical leg
Post-fix run (re-executed by US-361) of same fixture asserts: DriveDetector emits exactly 1 drive_id
Fixture wired into pytest -m "not slow" default sweep
Atlas reviewer sign-off recorded in PR description (no PM Rule 10 trigger -- test-only)
conditionalOutcomes: if unit-test harness alone cannot surface the defect, escalate to in-car drill with thread-id + emission-seam instrumentation per F-107 Step 1
if Drive 23/24 raw telemetry no longer accessible on chi-srv-01, derive synthetic per-second RPM/timestamp sequence from F-107 description constants

validationCriteria.action: Run pytest tests/pi/obdii/drive/test_dual_attribution_reproducer.py -v against pre-US-361 code
validationCriteria.outcome: Test FAILS with assertion: DriveDetector emitted 2 drive_ids on the replayed timing

validationCriteria.action: Run the same test post-US-361 fix
validationCriteria.outcome: Test PASSES: DriveDetector emitted exactly 1 drive_id

validationCriteria.action: Run full Pi unit-test sweep pytest tests/pi/ -m "not slow" post-US-361 fix
validationCriteria.outcome: All pre-existing single-drive tests stay GREEN

validationCriteria.action: Inspect fixture source
validationCriteria.outcome: Fixture is deterministic (clock-injected); no time.sleep or wall-clock dependency

stories.title: RCA -- git diff US-351 revert vs pre-US-349 + timing analysis
stories.goal: As a developer about to fix the DriveDetector dual-emission defect, I want a written RCA that names the file + line numbers in detector.py and lifecycle.py driving the second emission so that US-361 has a concrete fix target and Atlas has a load-bearing-subsystem RCA to gate against.
definitionOfDone: RCA document filed at offices/architect/findings/2026-05-28-drive-detector-dual-attribution-rca.md (or Ralph equivalent path) cross-linked into Atlas 2026-05-22 finding doc
RCA includes git diff sprint/sprint41-bugfixes-V0.27.17:src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py <commit-prior-to-US-348>:src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py output + interpretation
RCA names specific file:line locations in detector.py and/or lifecycle.py driving the second emission (per Atlas Refinements row 2)
RCA categorizes the defect: byte-identical revert + residual race | non-byte-identical revert with delta described | upstream V0.27.16 timing shift
RCA cross-referenced in US-361 fix commit message footer
conditionalOutcomes: if git diff shows US-351 revert was byte-identical to pre-US-349, RCA explains why pre-US-349 was emission-safe but V0.27.18 iteration isn't
if git diff shows revert was NOT byte-identical, RCA enumerates deltas line-by-line + identifies the race-opening delta
if RCA points to detector.py only, US-361 fix scope bounds to detector.py
if RCA points to lifecycle.py only or both modules, US-361 fix scope spans both modules

validationCriteria.action: Open RCA doc and search for detector.py: or lifecycle.py: followed by a line number
validationCriteria.outcome: At least one file:line reference appears in the RCA body

validationCriteria.action: Open RCA doc and search for the git diff invocation per F-107 Step 2
validationCriteria.outcome: The diff command + relevant output appears in the RCA body or is referenced as an artifact

validationCriteria.action: Open RCA doc and look for revert classification
validationCriteria.outcome: One of {byte-identical-residual-race, non-byte-identical-delta-described, upstream-timing-shift} explicitly named with supporting evidence

validationCriteria.action: Open US-361 commit message after fix lands
validationCriteria.outcome: Footer references the RCA doc path

stories.title: Root-cause fix: DriveDetector + lifecycle dual-emission prevention
stories.goal: As a developer closing the F-107 data-integrity defect, I want a root-cause fix to the Pi-side DriveDetector + drive lifecycle (both src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py AND src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py in scope per Q3 ruling) that prevents dual-emission of the same physical leg, validated behaviorally via the US-359 reproducer-fixture-passes-with-1-emission criterion.
definitionOfDone: Fix lands in detector.py and/or lifecycle.py per US-360 RCA disposition
US-359 reproducer fixture asserts exactly 1 drive_id emitted (FAIL pre-fix; PASS post-fix)
All pre-existing single-drive Pi unit tests stay GREEN (pytest tests/pi/ -m "not slow" clean)
No regression in B-104 Step 1 server-side compute path (server tests stay GREEN)
Pi-side drive_statistics table stays retired per US-351 (no resurrection)
Atlas Rule 10 sign-off recorded (load-bearing Pi subsystem touched)
Fix commit message footer references US-360 RCA doc
conditionalOutcomes: if US-360 RCA points to detector.py only, fix touches detector.py only (behavioral criterion still applies)
if US-360 RCA points to lifecycle.py only, fix touches lifecycle.py only
if US-360 RCA points to both modules, fix touches both
if a one-side fix satisfies V-1 but defense-in-depth in both modules would prevent future regression, prefer defense-in-depth + document rationale

validationCriteria.action: Run pytest tests/pi/obdii/drive/test_dual_attribution_reproducer.py -v post-fix
validationCriteria.outcome: PASSES: DriveDetector emits exactly 1 drive_id on Drive 23/24 timing replay

validationCriteria.action: Run full Pi unit-test sweep pytest tests/pi/ -m "not slow" post-fix
validationCriteria.outcome: All pre-existing single-drive tests stay GREEN; no regressions

validationCriteria.action: Run pytest tests/server/ -m "not slow" post-fix
validationCriteria.outcome: B-104 Step 1 compute path tests stay GREEN

validationCriteria.action: Grep for Pi-side drive_statistics import or table-creation references in src/pi/
validationCriteria.outcome: Zero references; table stays retired per US-351

validationCriteria.action: Atlas review of specs/architecture.md §10.7 amendment from US-373
validationCriteria.outcome: Atlas Rule 10 PASS recorded in §20 changelog table

stories.title: detect_overlapping_drives(session, drive_id) server compute helper
stories.goal: As a server-side analytics consumer of raw realtime_data, I want a detect_overlapping_drives(session, drive_id) -> list[int] helper that returns the set of other drive_ids overlapping the given drive_id by any second of time-range intersection so that downstream compute paths (US-363 + US-364) have a SSOT overlap detector.
definitionOfDone: Function detect_overlapping_drives(session, drive_id: int) -> list[int] exists in server compute path
Overlap threshold = strict time-range intersection per second (any second of overlap = match; ε allowance only with empirical justification documented in docstring)
Function operates against realtime_data (raw signal authority per B-104 Step 1), not drive_summary derived rows
Unit tests cover pairwise (drives 23+24), no-overlap (drive 25), transitive (3-way pairwise)
Function is idempotent (no DB writes; pure query helper)
Function documented with Google-style docstring per specs/standards.md
conditionalOutcomes: if production realtime_data for drives 23+24 is no longer accessible, unit tests must seed a fixture replicating overlap window from F-107 description constants
if 3-way transitive overlap surfaces in production, detect_overlapping_drives(30) returns [31,32] AND (31) returns [30,32] AND (32) returns [30,31]
if empirical ε > 0 second allowance is later needed (e.g., clock-jitter), the change is a separate Story with empirical justification

validationCriteria.action: Call detect_overlapping_drives(session, 23) against fixture seeded with drive 23+24 overlap
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns [24]

validationCriteria.action: Call detect_overlapping_drives(session, 25) against fixture with drive 25 + neighbors (no overlap)
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns []

validationCriteria.action: Call detect_overlapping_drives(session, 30) against 3-way overlap fixture (drives 30/31/32)
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns [31, 32] (order-insensitive; sorted preferred)

validationCriteria.action: Inspect docstring
validationCriteria.outcome: Overlap threshold = strict time-range intersection per second explicitly stated; ε=0 default call-out present

validationCriteria.action: Run helper against production realtime_data snapshot on chi-srv-01
validationCriteria.outcome: Drives 23+24 reported as overlapping each other; drives 25+ reported as not overlapping any neighbor

stories.title: Server tripwire: data_quality='attribution_anomaly' integration
stories.goal: As a server-side compute path producing drive_summary + drive_statistics rows, I want to invoke detect_overlapping_drives and stamp data_quality='attribution_anomaly' on rows whose drive_id has any overlap so that the V0.27.18 dual-attribution pattern is observable downstream as a per-row flag. The tripwire is observability not refusal: anomaly rows remain readable; consumers degrade gracefully.
definitionOfDone: Alembic v0010 substep extends data_quality enum / CHECK constraint to allow 'attribution_anomaly' on both drive_summary + drive_statistics
compute_drive_summary(drive_id) invokes detect_overlapping_drives; non-empty result -> writes 'attribution_anomaly'; empty -> 'full'
Same wiring on compute_drive_statistics(drive_id)
Downstream consumers verified: surface the flag visibly; never 500 / drop the row / refuse to render the rest
Unit tests: drive_id with overlap -> anomaly row; without overlap -> full row; downstream CLI consumer renders anomaly row gracefully
Idempotent: re-running compute on same drive_id produces identical row (per US-350 pattern)
Atlas Rule 10 sign-off recorded
conditionalOutcomes: if any downstream consumer hard-fails on the new enum value, patch the consumer before claiming completion (graceful degradation = DoD per Atlas Refinements row 5)
if a consumer chooses to FILTER anomaly rows by default, it MUST log a one-line WARNING per anomaly row (not silent suppression)

validationCriteria.action: Run compute_drive_summary(23) against fixture seeded with overlap
validationCriteria.outcome: drive_summary row written with data_quality='attribution_anomaly'

validationCriteria.action: Run compute_drive_summary(25) against fixture without overlap
validationCriteria.outcome: drive_summary row written with data_quality='full'

validationCriteria.action: Re-run compute_drive_summary(23) against same fixture
validationCriteria.outcome: Row content identical (idempotency check; zero-diff re-run)

validationCriteria.action: Run a representative downstream CLI consumer against drive 23
validationCriteria.outcome: Exit 0; output shows drive 23 row + [attribution_anomaly] marker; does not 500 / drop

validationCriteria.action: Inspect Alembic v0010 migration code
validationCriteria.outcome: Enum/CHECK extension for 'attribution_anomaly' applied to both drive_summary + drive_statistics

stories.title: Backfill drives 23+24 via recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id
stories.goal: As an operator closing historical data-integrity remediation on V0.27.18 IRL drill drives 23+24, I want a server-side recompute invocation that retroactively stamps data_quality='attribution_anomaly' on the affected drive_summary + drive_statistics rows, releasing regression_manifest HOLD on F-005 + F-007.
definitionOfDone: python -m server.cli.recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id 23 produces drive_summary + drive_statistics rows with data_quality='attribution_anomaly'
Same for --drive-id 24
Same for --drive-id 25 produces data_quality='full' (boundary check)
Idempotent re-run: zero diff on second invocation
regression_manifest HOLD on F-005 RELEASED on post-backfill verification
regression_manifest HOLD on F-007 RELEASED on post-backfill verification
Backfill outcome documented in offices/pm/knowledge/ or sprint deploy notes
conditionalOutcomes: if recompute is run before US-363 lands (out-of-order), anomaly stamping never fires; Ralph must verify sprint ordering
if more than one transitive-overlap cluster surfaces in historical data beyond drives 23+24, backfill SCOPE EXPANDS to include all overlap-cluster members

validationCriteria.action: Run python -m server.cli.recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id 23 against chi-srv-01 obd2db
validationCriteria.outcome: drive_summary + drive_statistics rows for drive 23 have data_quality='attribution_anomaly'

validationCriteria.action: Run python -m server.cli.recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id 24 against chi-srv-01
validationCriteria.outcome: Same anomaly stamping applied

validationCriteria.action: Run python -m server.cli.recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id 25 against chi-srv-01
validationCriteria.outcome: data_quality='full' (control)

validationCriteria.action: Re-run drive 23 + drive 24 invocations
validationCriteria.outcome: Zero row-diff; idempotency confirmed

validationCriteria.action: Inspect offices/pm/regression_manifest.json
validationCriteria.outcome: F-005 + F-007 moved from HELD to GREEN/released

validationCriteria.action: SELECT drive_id, data_quality FROM drive_summary WHERE drive_id IN (23, 24)
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns 2 rows, both with data_quality='attribution_anomaly'

stories.title: vehicle_info ECU + cal_signature columns + currently-active constraint
stories.goal: As a server-side analytics consumer needing per-ECU calibration awareness, I want vehicle_info to carry ecu_signature, cal_signature, ecu_install_timestamp_utc, ecu_removal_timestamp_utc columns with a constraint enforcing exactly one currently-active row so that downstream analytics can join per-drive data to the ECU active at the drive's time window.
definitionOfDone: Alembic v0010 substep adds vehicle_info columns: ecu_signature TEXT NOT NULL, cal_signature TEXT NULL, ecu_install_timestamp_utc TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, ecu_removal_timestamp_utc TIMESTAMP NULL
Constraint enforces exactly one row with NULL ecu_removal_timestamp_utc (partial unique index or equivalent MariaDB-supported shape)
Migration path for pre-existing vehicle_info rows: backfill with prior-ECU signature (per US-367 data; PM choice over NULL+pre-tracking-semantic per Atlas Refinements row 7)
ORM model (src/server/models/vehicle_info.py or equivalent) updated to reflect new shape
Atlas Rule 10 sign-off recorded (load-bearing schema change)
conditionalOutcomes: if any pre-existing vehicle_info row predates 2026-05-22 swap, backfill writes prior-ECU signature with ecu_removal_timestamp_utc = 2026-05-22 swap timestamp (not NULL)
if Alembic detects > 1 row with NULL ecu_removal_timestamp_utc post-backfill, migration FAILS LOUDLY (invariant violated; do not silently choose a winner)
if MariaDB version doesn't support partial unique indexes, implement via CHECK + app-layer enforcement; document choice in migration docstring

validationCriteria.action: DESCRIBE vehicle_info on chi-srv-01 post-migration
validationCriteria.outcome: Shows ecu_signature, cal_signature, ecu_install_timestamp_utc, ecu_removal_timestamp_utc with correct types + NULL semantics

validationCriteria.action: INSERT vehicle_info with valid ecu_signature + NULL removal_timestamp when zero rows are currently-active
validationCriteria.outcome: Insert succeeds

validationCriteria.action: INSERT vehicle_info with valid ecu_signature + NULL removal_timestamp when one row already has NULL removal_timestamp
validationCriteria.outcome: Insert FAILS with constraint violation

validationCriteria.action: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM vehicle_info WHERE ecu_removal_timestamp_utc IS NULL post-backfill
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns exactly 1

validationCriteria.action: Inspect pre-existing vehicle_info rows post-migration
validationCriteria.outcome: ecu_signature populated (prior-ECU backfill) + ecu_removal_timestamp_utc set to 2026-05-22 swap timestamp

validationCriteria.action: ORM smoke-test: load + serialize a vehicle_info row
validationCriteria.outcome: New columns present in serialized output; round-trip clean

stories.title: stamp_ecu_swap + show_ecu_lineage server CLIs
stories.goal: As CIO recording a future ECU swap, I want a stamp_ecu_swap CLI that atomically closes the currently-active vehicle_info row + opens a new row, and a show_ecu_lineage CLI that lists all historical stamps so that the swap-history audit trail stays maintainable from the command line.
definitionOfDone: CLI python -m server.cli.stamp_ecu_swap --signature <id> --cal-signature <id-or-null> --as-of <ts> records a swap event (close prior row + insert new row)
CLI python -m server.cli.show_ecu_lineage lists all historical ECU stamps (text table, sortable by install timestamp; current row highlighted)
Idempotency: re-running stamp_ecu_swap with same signature + as-of is no-op (PM choice per Atlas Refinements row 8; cleaner UX than fail-loudly)
Atomicity: close + open executed in single DB transaction; partial-failure rollback preserves invariant
Unit tests cover: clean swap; idempotent re-run; pre-migration graceful error; bootstrap (no currently-active row) graceful error
Documentation for CIO + Spool usage
conditionalOutcomes: if stamp_ecu_swap re-runs with same --signature + --as-of, exit 0 with INFO 'no-op' (idempotent)
if stamp_ecu_swap runs with same --signature but DIFFERENT --as-of, exit non-zero with ERROR (prevents silent timestamp rewrite)
if show_ecu_lineage runs against empty vehicle_info, exit 0 with 'no ECU lineage recorded yet'

validationCriteria.action: Run stamp_ecu_swap --signature 'new-test-ECU' --cal-signature 'tune-v1' --as-of '2026-06-01T12:00:00Z' against fixture with one currently-active row
validationCriteria.outcome: Currently-active row's ecu_removal_timestamp_utc set to 2026-06-01T12:00:00Z; new row inserted with signature='new-test-ECU' + NULL removal timestamp

validationCriteria.action: Re-run V-1 with identical args
validationCriteria.outcome: Exit 0; INFO 'no-op'; database state unchanged

validationCriteria.action: Run stamp_ecu_swap with same signature but different --as-of timestamp
validationCriteria.outcome: Exit non-zero; ERROR; database state unchanged

validationCriteria.action: Run show_ecu_lineage
validationCriteria.outcome: Output lists both rows (closed + currently-active) with timestamps + signatures in install-timestamp order

validationCriteria.action: Inspect stamp_ecu_swap source code
validationCriteria.outcome: Close + open execute in a single DB transaction

validationCriteria.action: Run show_ecu_lineage against empty vehicle_info fixture
validationCriteria.outcome: Exit 0; 'no ECU lineage recorded yet' message

stories.title: Backfill 2 ECU rows (prior + 2026-05-22 swap) + join verification
stories.goal: As a server-side analytics consumer wanting per-drive ECU attribution, I want the historical ECU lineage backfilled into vehicle_info (prior ECU active before 2026-05-22 + new modified-EPROM ECU active from 2026-05-22 onward) so that any drive's start_time_utc can join to the active ECU at that time.
definitionOfDone: Backfill seeds exactly 2 rows: prior ECU (signature per Spool sign-off; install ts from CIO records; removal ts = 2026-05-22 swap) + new ECU (signature per F-108; install ts = 2026-05-22; removal ts = NULL)

Data sources documented in commit body: prior-ECU install timestamp source (Eclipse 1998 Projects.gsheet or earliest realtime_data.captured_at -- approximate but documented); 2026-05-22 swap timestamp precise per chain-merge history
Spool sign-off on signature naming convention recorded BEFORE backfill (per F-108 last bullet)
Backfill executed via stamp_ecu_swap CLI OR one-shot backfill script for first-row bootstrap

Join verification: SELECT drive_summary.drive_id, vehicle_info.ecu_signature FROM drive_summary JOIN vehicle_info ON drive_summary.start_time_utc BETWEEN install AND COALESCE(removal, NOW()) correctly partitions drives 1-24 (prior) + drives 25+ (new)
conditionalOutcomes: if Spool has not signed off on signature naming convention, BLOCK the backfill + route A2AL note to Spool
if stamp_ecu_swap cannot stamp the first row (bootstrap edge case), a one-shot backfill script handles the first row
if join verification shows any drive 1-24 partitioning to new ECU (or any drive 25+ partitioning to prior ECU), revise + re-backfill

validationCriteria.action: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM vehicle_info post-backfill
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns 2

validationCriteria.action: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM vehicle_info WHERE ecu_removal_timestamp_utc IS NULL post-backfill
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns 1 (currently-active new ECU)

validationCriteria.action: Run join verification SELECT against drive_summary for drives 1-24
validationCriteria.outcome: All rows JOIN to the prior-ECU row

validationCriteria.action: Run join verification SELECT against drive_summary for drives 25+
validationCriteria.outcome: All rows JOIN to the new-ECU row

validationCriteria.action: Inspect commit body
validationCriteria.outcome: Both data sources documented: prior-ECU install timestamp source named (gsheet or realtime_data); 2026-05-22 swap timestamp precise + cross-referenced

validationCriteria.action: Inspect Spool's inbox or offices/pm/inbox/
validationCriteria.outcome: Spool sign-off note on naming convention exists with date before backfill commit

stories.title: dtc_freeze_frame table + Pi-side Mode 02 capture on MIL_ON
stories.goal: As a tuner doing post-mortem on a DTC trigger, I want Mode 02 freeze-frame (16 PIDs mirroring Mode 01) captured + stored alongside the DTC log entry so that forensic analysis has 'what was the engine doing when this error tripped' context. Per Q4: dtc_freeze_frame carries FK to vehicle_info.id (specific row); immutability via vehicle_info append-only invariant.
definitionOfDone: Alembic v0010 substep creates dtc_freeze_frame table: id PK; dtc_log_id FK -> dtc_log.id; captured_at_timestamp_utc; pid_responses_json JSON; vehicle_info_id FK -> vehicle_info.id
vehicle_info table comment documents append-only invariant: corrections via close-prior-row + open-new-row; never UPDATE a row a freeze-frame points to
Append-only invariant enforced via writer-path discipline (no UPDATE statements on vehicle_info in src/server/; corrections through stamp_ecu_swap)
Regression test: writer-path discipline asserted (grep test for no UPDATE on vehicle_info OR runtime assertion)
Pi-side: MIL_ON event triggers Mode 02 enumeration of 16 PIDs; writes single row keyed by (dtc_log_id, captured_at) with vehicle_info_id resolved to currently-active row at capture time
Pi-side unit test: synthetic MIL_ON triggers Mode 02 capture path; row written with 16-PID JSON + correct FK
Atlas Rule 10 sign-off recorded
conditionalOutcomes: if Spool later vetoes append-only invariant (Q4 veto path), pivot to FK + denormalized ecu_signature_at_capture TEXT; separate Story for V0.28.1
if OBD simulator under simulate mode doesn't return Mode 02 responses, add Mode 02 PID enumeration to simulator (in-scope)
if DTC trips but Mode 02 PIDs unavailable, write row with pid_responses_json={} + notes field explaining gap (graceful degradation)

validationCriteria.action: DESCRIBE dtc_freeze_frame on chi-srv-01 post-migration
validationCriteria.outcome: Shows columns: id, dtc_log_id (FK), captured_at_timestamp_utc, pid_responses_json, vehicle_info_id (FK)

validationCriteria.action: Trigger synthetic MIL_ON in Pi unit-test fixture
validationCriteria.outcome: dtc_freeze_frame row written with 16-PID JSON; vehicle_info_id resolved to currently-active row at capture time

validationCriteria.outcome: Append-only invariant explicitly documented

validationCriteria.outcome: Tests pass; MIL_ON -> Mode 02 -> row write path covered

validationCriteria.action: Trigger MIL_ON with Mode 02 PIDs unavailable (simulator drops them)
validationCriteria.outcome: Row still written with pid_responses_json={} + notes; no crash

stories.title: Server sync + show_dtc_freeze_frame CLI
stories.goal: As an operator wanting to inspect a freeze-frame after a DTC trigger, I want Pi-to-server sync to deliver dtc_freeze_frame rows + a show_dtc_freeze_frame --dtc-log-id N CLI that prints the 16-PID dictionary + joins through to the correct vehicle_info row by FK (Q4 round-trip) so that forensic context is available without raw-SQL.
definitionOfDone: Pi-to-server sync maps Pi-side dtc_freeze_frame rows to server-side rows; round-trip preserves all 16 PIDs + FKs

python -m server.cli.show_dtc_freeze_frame --dtc-log-id N prints: dtc_log row (DTC code + ts) + dtc_freeze_frame row (captured_at + 16 PIDs) + vehicle_info row joined via FK (ecu_signature, cal_signature, install/removal timestamps)
Q4 round-trip: CLI correctly joins through to vehicle_info row active at capture time, even after stamp_ecu_swap has added new rows
Sync delivery test: synthetic Pi row syncs to server; CLI returns synced row with 16 PIDs intact
CLI handles missing freeze-frame gracefully: exit 0 with 'no freeze-frame recorded for this DTC'
conditionalOutcomes: if sync delivers dtc_freeze_frame row whose vehicle_info_id FK doesn't exist server-side, sync FAILS LOUDLY (cross-tier-ID issue logged; no silent re-resolve)
if pid_responses_json is empty (graceful degradation from US-368 V-6), CLI displays 'freeze-frame captured but Mode 02 PIDs unavailable' + notes field
if multiple freeze-frame rows exist for one dtc_log_id (defensive), CLI displays most recent by captured_at + WARN

validationCriteria.outcome: Server-side row exists with all 16 PIDs + FKs preserved

stories.title: speed_pid_calibration table + 2-ECU seed; FK to vehicle_info.ecu_signature
stories.goal: As a server-side analytics consumer aggregating SPEED PID for distance / gear-inference, I want a speed_pid_calibration table keyed by ecu_signature (FK to vehicle_info) carrying a multiplicative correction_factor so that the new modified-EPROM ECU's ~2x SPEED-reading drift gets corrected per-drive. NOTE: new-ECU seed correction_factor=0.5 is INITIAL ESTIMATE; Spool empirical refinement Q2 PENDING.
definitionOfDone: Alembic v0010 substep creates speed_pid_calibration table per F-076 V0.28 §1 schema: id PK; ecu_signature FK -> vehicle_info; correction_factor DOUBLE; capture_method ENUM; captured_at_timestamp_utc; captured_by; notes
Seed 2 rows: prior ECU correction_factor=1.0 (Drive 18 gear math fit); new ECU correction_factor=0.5 (INITIAL ESTIMATE per Drive 26; pending Q2)
notes field documents initial estimate flag for new-ECU seed
Ordering: US-365 substep runs BEFORE US-370 substep in v0010 (US-370 FKs to US-365's column); documented in migration docstring per Atlas Refinements row 16
Atlas Rule 10 sign-off recorded
conditionalOutcomes: if Spool answers Q2 with different new-ECU correction_factor before sprint freeze, seed value updated in this Story (not new Story)
if Spool answers Q2 deferring to GPS-correlation drive, new-ECU row seeds correction_factor=0.5 + capture_method='default' + notes='INITIAL ESTIMATE pending GPS-correlation drive Story TBD'

if SPEED-PID consumer query is touched in this sprint, it MUST JOIN through vehicle_info.ecu_signature -> speed_pid_calibration -> apply correction_factor before integrating; consumer changes optional in this Story

validationCriteria.outcome: Shows columns per F-076 V0.28 §1 schema with FK to vehicle_info

validationCriteria.outcome: Returns 1.0

validationCriteria.action: SELECT correction_factor FROM speed_pid_calibration WHERE ecu_signature = '<new-ECU-sig>'
validationCriteria.outcome: Returns 0.5 (initial estimate; subject to Q2 refinement)

stories.title: Column rename: drive_statistics.drive_id -> summary_id + consumer updates
stories.goal: As a server-side analytics consumer reading drive_statistics, I want the misleading drive_id column renamed to summary_id (which is what the column actually holds: a drive_summary.id FK, NOT a Pi-assigned drive_id) so that future authors aren't lied to about the column's meaning. Rename is COMPLETE not additive: no deprecated alias.
definitionOfDone: Alembic v0010 substep: ALTER TABLE drive_statistics RENAME COLUMN drive_id TO summary_id (server-side; Pi-side retired in US-351)
ORM model updated: src/server/models/drive_statistics.py references summary_id

All consumers grepped + updated: src/server/analytics/drive_statistics_compute.py; src/server/cli/recompute_drive_analytics.py; src/server/reports/*.py; src/server/services/analysis.py; tests/server/; Spool's ad-hoc SQL in offices/tuner/
Post-rename: SELECT summary_id FROM drive_statistics LIMIT 1 succeeds; SELECT drive_id FROM drive_statistics LIMIT 1 FAILS (no alias)
Existing analytics queries return same results post-rename (semantic-equivalent rewrites)
Atlas Rule 10 sign-off recorded
conditionalOutcomes: if grep surfaces a consumer referencing drive_statistics.drive_id outside src/server/ (e.g., offices/tuner/ or docs/), route A2AL note to owning agent + update reference
if MariaDB doesn't support RENAME COLUMN cleanly, fallback to ADD summary_id + UPDATE + DROP drive_id in same substep (still no alias)

stories.title: drive_summary.drive_id NULL decision + implementation
stories.goal: As a server-side analytics consumer reading drive_summary, I want the drive_id NULL smell resolved via Q1 option (a) Backfill + invariant so that drive_summary.drive_id is semantically meaningful going forward + future authors aren't bitten by NULL. Q1 ruling 2026-05-28: backfill from source_id + CHECK invariant; SSOT-purist drop deferred to V0.28+ broader normalization.
definitionOfDone: Alembic v0010 substep in order: (i) UPDATE drive_summary SET drive_id = source_id WHERE drive_id IS NULL AND source_id IS NOT NULL; (ii) ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT chk_drive_id_source_id CHECK ((drive_id IS NULL AND source_id IS NULL) OR (drive_id = source_id))

Writer-path discipline: server-side src/server/analytics/drive_summary_compute.py AND Pi-side src/pi/.../drive_summary.py set BOTH drive_id AND source_id on INSERT/UPSERT (never one-without-the-other when known)
Regression test asserts invariant under both write paths: server compute INSERT -> both set + equal; Pi write INSERT -> both set + equal; direct INSERT violating CHECK FAILS
Substep ordering documented in v0010 migration docstring: UPDATE BEFORE ALTER (per Atlas Refinements row 16)
Atlas Rule 10 sign-off recorded
conditionalOutcomes: if any pre-existing row has drive_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NULL (asymmetric), migration UPDATEs such rows to source_id=drive_id BEFORE adding CHECK; OR enumerate + manually disposition
if a writer-path code site only knows one column at write time (e.g., legacy payload with only source_id), write (drive_id=source_id, source_id=source_id) so the invariant holds

validationCriteria.outcome: Returns 0 (Q1 backfill invariant; Atlas Sprint-IRL clause #5)

validationCriteria.outcome: Resulting row has drive_id=11 AND source_id=11

validationCriteria.outcome: Resulting row has drive_id=source_id (both columns set to same Pi-assigned id)
